meaning: which side the bag is being carried on relative to the reference foot
input_output_role: output of DEX1 and input of DEX2
evidence: notation writes SAME/OP as a dex output and on the terminal; the intermediate carriage has no token, so it is recoverable only if a per-dex transition function is assumed
consequence_if_omitted: composability is undefined: cannot tell whether DEX2 can engage the bag where DEX1 left it

meaning: which foot the SAME/OP frame is measured against
input_output_role: frame for the side coordinate of both dexes
evidence: the positional-identity doctrine fixes side against a reference foot but never states which foot it is or whether it is invariant across a chain
consequence_if_omitted: SAME/OP is uninterpretable across the chain; the second cube's side axis may be re-coordinatized (the product becomes a twisted bundle)

meaning: which leg is planted (support) and which is free to circle
input_output_role: input requirement of DEX2 (needs a free circling leg)
evidence: a dexterity is defined as one leg circling while the other supports, so a free leg is a precondition; no per-dex leg token exists
consequence_if_omitted: cannot tell whether DEX2's circling leg is available; some pairs demand a leg DEX1 left as support

input_output_role: input and output; gates leg availability and the receiver relationship
evidence: posture markers appear per step in notation but not on every step (they are sporadic)
consequence_if_omitted: cannot distinguish a planted handoff from an airborne one; the set of legal continuations changes with stance

meaning: whether the receiving foot is far or near at the handoff
evidence: the conditional grade fires on a far receiver; far-ness is a function of carriage_side and reference_foot, not an independent variable
consequence_if_omitted: grade non-separability becomes invisible; if far-ness also gates well-formedness (unresolved) some pairs silently fail

meaning: how high the bag is / where in its flight it is
evidence: no height token exists in the notation; whether height is a structural or a physical variable is itself unsettled
consequence_if_omitted: if structurally required, some same-side same-carriage pairs still fail; but this may belong to the physical layer, not the grammar

meaning: the direction and spin of the bag in flight
evidence: no trajectory token exists; likely a physical-layer variable
consequence_if_omitted: same status as vertical phase: possibly a physical constraint the grammar does not and should not encode

meaning: the body's rotational orientation between dexes
input_output_role: input and output in spinning contexts
evidence: a body spin is a tokenized operator, but continuous rotational phase between dexes is not
consequence_if_omitted: spinning handoffs are under-determined; the phase a spin leaves is not recoverable from the tokens

meaning: whether a contact crosses the body
encoding_status: not of a pure dex-to-dex handoff
confidence: the cross-body token marks a terminal or a contact; there is no contact between two chained dexes, so it does not describe the handoff
column8: none for the pure dex-to-dex handoff; relevant only if an intermediate contact is inserted

meaning: whether DEX2 follows DEX1 in sequence or overlaps it
evidence: the sequence and layered-step operators are tokenized, but the scoring of the layered case is unresolved in the grammar
consequence_if_omitted: cannot distinguish a sequential from a simultaneous two-dex movement; the scoring of the layered reading is undefined

input_output_role: output of DEX2 at the endpoint (not a handoff variable)
evidence: the terminal token carries surface and side
consequence_if_omitted: none: it is the observable endpoint, always recorded
